title: NVTS Stock Rockets As AI Power Pivot Ignites Analyst Upgrades
text: Navitas Semiconductor Corporation (NASDAQ: NVTS) surged 4.91% on the trading day, with the stock climbing from $13.20 in late April to the low-$20s by mid-May, including individual jumps of 17.1% and 22.2%. The chipmaker reported Q1 revenue of $8.6 million (beating the $8.18M estimate) and posted an EPS of -$0.04 versus consensus loss of -$0.05. Management guided Q2 2026 revenue toward approximately $10 million with gross margins approaching 39%. Needham raised its price target from $13 to $21 with a Buy rating, while Baird more than doubled its target from $9 to $20. However, Morgan Stanley raised its NVTS target from $4.20 to $12.50 while remaining Underweight and consensus remains Hold at $13.59. The company is transitioning from low-margin mobile chargers to high-power GaN and SiC products tied to AI data-center power demands. A licensing deal with Cyient Semiconductors for India's GaN IC family signals ecosystem expansion. Despite momentum, NVTS remains unprofitable with Q1 operating losses exceeding $27 million and a price-to-sales ratio above 100.
